home_h2_7: What our patients say about us...
home_h3_9:
Specialist in fetal medicine and pregnancy ultrasound
Personal, professional and compassionate support throughout pregnancy  From first heartbeat to birth - in the life before life.
home_p_26: In 2021-2023, Dr. Harak completed a clinical subspecialty at BCNatal Fetal Medicine in Barcelona, one of the world's leading fetal medicine centers. This training provided him with rich experience and advanced diagnostic techniques.
home_p_27: Rabin-Beilinson Medical Center, Petah Tikva, Israel
home_p_28: Dr. Harak leads the fetal neurology department at Rabin-Beilinson Medical Center, specializing in targeted fetal brain scans and research related to fetal anomalies with emphasis on the central nervous system.
home_p_30: At our clinic we offer anatomy scans, nuchal translucency tests and monitoring of high-risk pregnancies, combining the most advanced ultrasound technology, rich clinical experience and a personal, sensitive and reassuring approach for every woman and every couple.
home_p_32: From the first meeting until birth – Life Inside Clinic offers a personalized examination pathway that combines advanced technology with a warm and humane approach. Every stage is designed to provide you with the information, confidence and peace of mind you deserve.
home_p_33: In the most sensitive stage of pregnancy, I accompany you with precise and reassuring examinations that confirm fetal development and enable early detection of any abnormal findings.
home_p_34: The private anatomy scan in Raanana is performed using the most advanced ultrasound technology, with meticulous examination of all fetal body systems, including brain, heart, internal organs and limbs.
home_p_35: In the third trimester we continue to accompany you with targeted scans and close monitoring, especially in high-risk pregnancies, to ensure optimal fetal development towards birth.
home_p_37: Real experiences of our patients from our clinic in Raanana
home_p_39: I wanted to share an excellent and particularly reassuring experience. We came to the examination with quite a few concerns, and left with a feeling of confidence and calm. Dr. Ron Harak is a very professional, thorough and precise physician, who dedicates real time to the examination and is meticulous in every detail. Beyond the impressive professionalism, he explains with great patience, answers every question calmly and at eye level, and gives the feeling that you are truly seen. We felt care, sensitivity and consideration throughout all the examinations he performed and this is not taken for granted. I warmly recommend to anyone looking for a thorough, humane and reassuring doctor during such a significant period. Thank you very much for professional and humane care alike.
home_p_41: I had a first scan with Dr. Ron Harak after receiving warm recommendations from friends. He was super professional, knew his work in an inspiring way, explained along the way and made me feel very comfortable and that I was in good hands. I highly recommend to anyone who wants to get peace of mind about their pregnancy to go to Dr. Ron.
home_p_43: We came for a third trimester anatomy scan and simply enjoyed it! Beyond the experience of a couple seeing their fetus, we felt comfortable, received treatment at eye level and professional, educational and interesting explanations so we could understand what we were seeing! The desire for us to be comfortable and get the most out of the meeting was completely felt. Highly recommend and hope to return!
home_p_45: I am so happy that I came to Dr. Harak who is accompanying me during pregnancy in these months. He is professional, kind, patient and extremely pleasant. Without a doubt the most humane and pleasant doctor I have ever met! Dr. Harak gives the feeling that he is here for me and that there is room to bring anxieties and concerns without being embarrassed or apologizing. I warmly recommend coming to him for a private scan. The examination is very thorough and professional, accompanied by explanations and there is always room to ask questions. I warmly recommend with all my heart!
home_p_47: The first thing you meet is the lovely Iris who explains everything with a bright face. After that, Dr. Ron came out of the room and approached to greet us. Something I have never encountered in Israel. He didn't sit behind the chair and computer but got up to greet me. He performed a detailed scan and didn't spare anything. I felt that I was in the best possible hands and this alongside a smile and humanity and endless explanations. I warmly recommend
home_p_49:
We had an excellent experience! 
This is a professional doctor at the highest level possible! Thorough, fluent and clear in his explanations. Beyond that, he is pleasant, warm and instills a sense of confidence throughout the entire examination. I felt that I was in the best hands - I wouldn't choose anyone else. A perfect experience in every respect, highly recommend!
home_p_51: Life Inside clinic in Ra'anana provides a fascinating glimpse into the life before life and strives to provide every pregnant woman with an environment where the most advanced medical information is delivered with clarity, warmth and full personal care.
home_p_52: Dr. Ron Harak explains the importance of early detection of fetal developmental disorders.
home_p_54: The clinic uses the world's most advanced ultrasound equipment, enabling exceptionally high-level fetal diagnosis with outstanding imaging quality, clinical precision, and a personal and meticulous examination experience.
home_p_55: Every finding is explained in simple and understandable language. We believe that accurate information, conveyed with transparency and patience, reduces anxiety and enables you to make informed decisions throughout your pregnancy.
home_p_56: Pregnancy is a profound emotional journey. We are here not only as doctors, but as full partners on the journey from the first examination until birth, with accessibility, patience and personal attention to each couple.
home_p_57: We offer comprehensive and personal care for all stages of pregnancy and a fascinating glimpse into the life before life.  Dr. Ron Harak will be happy to be at your service.
home_p_58: Dr. Ron Harak | Life Before Life

contact_h1_1: We'd Love to Hear from You
contact_h2_4: How to Prepare for Your Clinic Visit
contact_p_15: Life Inside clinic in Ra'anana offers a private, personal, and calm environment for all stages of pregnancy — from routine to complex. Dr. Ron Harak will provide you with professional, warm, and precise answers to any question in the field of fetal medicine, obstetric ultrasound, and private anatomy scan in Ra'anana.
contact_p_16: Life Inside clinic is located in Ra'anana and offers the highest level fetal medicine and obstetric ultrasound services in a private, warm, and personal environment.
contact_p_17:
2 HaNofar Street, Mantzur Building 1
Ra'anana, Israel
contact_p_19: A private and personal clinic designed to provide a calm and supportive environment. Each appointment is conducted in complete privacy, without the hustle and bustle of a public clinic — with full time and complete attention for each patient.
contact_p_20: Private anatomy scan in Ra'anana, nuchal translucency screening, third trimester scan, prenatal diagnosis, high-risk pregnancies, and fetal neurology — all under one roof with a leading specialist.
contact_p_21: The clinic is conveniently accessible to patients from the entire central region — Ra'anana, Kfar Saba, Herzliya, Netanya, and Tel Aviv. Ample parking available near the address.
contact_p_22: Appointment scheduling by phone or through the contact form
contact_p_23: Feel free to leave your details and we'll get back to you promptly to schedule a consultation, private anatomy scan in Ra'anana, or for any question regarding clinic services. Dr. Ron Harak and the clinic team are here for you.
contact_p_24: Your details are kept with us in complete confidentiality. We'll get back to you within one business day.
contact_p_25: To provide you with the most accurate and personalized care, we've prepared some simple guidelines for you before your visit.
contact_p_26: When contacting us, please specify your current pregnancy week, reason for referral, and if you received a referral from another healthcare provider — their details will help us prepare the most suitable examination for you. You may also mention previous findings or tests that were performed.
contact_p_27: For private anatomy scan in Ra'anana and most ultrasound examinations, no prior fasting is required. It's recommended to arrive with a partially full bladder for early examinations, and to bring your pregnancy booklet and documentation of previous tests for continuous and accurate care.
contact_p_28: Life Inside clinic is located at 2 HaNofar Street, Mantzur Building 1, Ra'anana. For appointment scheduling, additional questions, or any clarification — we're here for you. The clinic team maintains personal follow-up and is available for any inquiry with warmth and promptness.
contact_p_29: For any additional questions, to schedule a private nuchal translucency screening in Ra'anana, or for a third trimester scan — we invite you to contact us directly. Dr. Ron Harak and the clinic team will be happy to assist you.

late_review_h2_2: What is the Late Anatomy Scan?
late_review_h2_3: Fetal Heart — Central Focus of the Scan
late_review_h3_6: The late anatomy scan is a comprehensive ultrasound examination, typically performed between weeks 21–24 of pregnancy. The purpose of the examination is to systematically evaluate the fetal structure and development of body systems at a more advanced stage of pregnancy.
late_review_h3_7: The examination includes assessment of the brain, heart, spine, face, limbs, kidneys, digestive system and additional organs. The scan enables identification of structural findings that may appear or become clearer only later in pregnancy.
late_review_h3_8: Additionally, it's possible to assess fetal growth, amniotic fluid volume, placental location and sometimes perform blood flow evaluation according to clinical need.
late_review_h3_9: Dr. Charak performs the scan with a professional, thorough and sensitive approach, using advanced ultrasound equipment that enables high-quality and clear imaging. During the examination, emphasis is placed on clear, patient and personalized explanation, to provide parents with a sense of confidence and complete understanding.
late_review_p_14: Fetal heart assessment is one of the most complex and important challenges in the late anatomy scan. Dr. Charak performs a comprehensive echocardiographic examination of heart structure using advanced imaging techniques.
late_review_p_15: Contact Life Inside clinic in Raanana to schedule an appointment with Dr. Ron Charak. We are here to answer any question and provide you with peace of mind throughout pregnancy.
late_review_p_16: Dr. Ron Charak | The Life Before Life

about_dr_ron_h2_2:
Specialist in Fetal Medicine
and Obstetric Ultrasound
about_dr_ron_h2_4: Ready to start the journey together?
about_dr_ron_h3_6: Joyce and Irving Goldman Medical School, Ben-Gurion University of the Negev
about_dr_ron_p_14:
Dr. Ron Harak is married and a father of two 
Senior physician and director of the Fetal Neurology Clinic at Rabin Medical Center - Beilinson.
 With extensive professional experience, broad education and commitment to medical excellence, Dr. Harak is among the leaders in Israel and worldwide in the field of fetal medicine and obstetric ultrasound.

Background and Education
Dr. Harak is a distinguished graduate of the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine at Ben-Gurion University, and holds a Master's degree in Health Systems Management from the School of Business Administration at Ben-Gurion University. In 2018, he completed his residency in Obstetrics and Gynecology at Soroka Hospital with highest honors, and subsequently deepened his expertise in obstetric and gynecological ultrasound as a senior physician at Hadassah Hospital in Jerusalem.

International Fellowship
Between 2021-2023, Dr. Harak completed a clinical fellowship in fetal medicine and obstetric ultrasound at BCNatal Fetal Medicine Center in Barcelona, one of the world's leading centers in fetal medicine. This period allowed him to acquire advanced tools and techniques that place him at the forefront of medical innovation.

Areas of Expertise
Upon his return to Israel, Dr. Harak was appointed as a senior physician in the Imaging Unit for Obstetrics, Gynecology and Fetal Medicine at Rabin Medical Center - Beilinson, where he leads the field of fetal neurology and manages the specialized clinic for treating complex cases related to the fetal brain. 
His areas of practice include:
Treatment of high-risk pregnancies
Prenatal diagnosis
Research on fetal anomalies, with emphasis on the fetal brain
Targeted fetal brain scans

Dr. Harak combines in his clinical and research work technical expertise alongside a humane and personal approach to patients. He strives to provide the most accurate and advanced treatment, while maintaining close and supportive contact with patients.
With a medical vision and constant pursuit of excellence, Dr. Harak provides comprehensive and thorough care for every pregnant woman.
about_dr_ron_p_16: A Path of Excellence — From Academia to the Forefront of Fetal Medicine

late_review_alt_66: Life Inside Clinic – Beit Manzur Raanana
late_review_btn_41: How long does the scan take?
late_review_btn_42: What if not all structures are visible?
late_review_btn_43: Is special preparation required for the scan?
late_review_div_50: A complete late review typically takes about an hour. The duration of the examination depends on fetal position, amniotic fluid volume and complexity of findings. Dr. Charak ensures not to finish the examination until all structures are optimally visualized.
late_review_div_51: Sometimes, due to fetal position, abdominal structure or amniotic fluid volume, it's not possible to see all structures in one examination. In such cases, Dr. Charak will document which structures were not visible and schedule a complementary examination at no additional charge.
late_review_div_52: The late review does not require special preparation. There is no need to arrive with a full bladder. It is recommended to eat a light meal about an hour before the examination — sometimes fetal activity after eating helps obtain better images. It is advisable to arrive in comfortable clothing and bring previous examination findings and the pregnancy book.
late_review_nav_59: Third Trimester Review for Fetal Development Assessment

clinic_services_h2_2: Early Pregnancy Tests for Safe Pregnancy
clinic_services_h2_4:
Comprehensive Follow-up and Advanced
Prenatal Diagnosis
clinic_services_h2_5: What to Expect at Our Clinic
clinic_services_h2_6: Which Examination is Right for Your Stage of Pregnancy?
clinic_services_p_26: Life Inside Clinic offers a comprehensive range of obstetric ultrasound and fetal medicine examinations in a private, personal, and professional environment. Under the guidance of Dr. Ron Harel — a senior physician and director of the Fetal Neurology Clinic at Rabin-Beilinson Medical Center — every examination is performed with advanced equipment, experienced eyes, and a warm heart. Whether it's a private anatomy scan in Ra'anana for the first time, private nuchal translucency screening in Ra'anana, or high-risk pregnancy monitoring — you are in the best hands.
clinic_services_p_27: Confirmation of normal pregnancy and detection of the fetus's first heartbeats in the early stages of pregnancy.
clinic_services_p_28: Private nuchal translucency screening in Ra'anana — early genetic screening at weeks 11–14, including accurate risk assessment.
clinic_services_p_29: Detailed anatomical examination at weeks 14–16, enabling early identification of significant findings.
clinic_services_p_30: Private anatomy scan in Ra'anana — comprehensive anatomical examination at weeks 20–24 with emphasis on the fetal brain and internal organs.
clinic_services_p_31: Growth assessment, placental location, and amniotic fluid levels in the third trimester, including fetal well-being evaluation.
clinic_services_p_32: Targeted scans for the fetal brain, Doppler studies, and high-risk pregnancy monitoring with complete personalization.
clinic_services_p_34: In the early stages of pregnancy, two key examinations provide vital information about fetal development and allow us to give you peace of mind as early as possible. The examinations are performed at Life Inside clinic in Ra'anana under advanced imaging conditions with warm personal guidance from Dr. Harel.
clinic_services_p_36: The first examination that confirms everything is starting properly. Using gentle transvaginal ultrasound, we locate the gestational sac, verify its position in the uterus, and confirm the presence of normal fetal heartbeat.
clinic_services_p_38:
Nuchal translucency screening is one of the important examinations in early pregnancy, usually performed between weeks 11-13+6. During the examination, the thickness of fluid in the fetal neck area is measured, combined with assessment of gestational age, heartbeat, initial fetal structure, and sometimes additional markers.

The purpose of the examination is to assess the risk of chromosomal disorders, including Down syndrome, and to detect early findings that may indicate the need for additional monitoring or investigation.

At the clinic, the examination is performed with a thorough, sensitive, and professional approach, using particularly advanced ultrasound equipment that enables clear and accurate imaging even in the early stages of pregnancy. Alongside the medical aspect, emphasis is placed on clear explanation, answering questions, and providing a calm and safe experience for the couple. The examination is suitable for women in early pregnancy who want early, accurate, and professional assessment of fetal development.
clinic_services_p_39: Detailed and accurate examinations throughout pregnancy, combining advanced technology with clinical experience
clinic_services_p_40:
Early anatomy scan is an important and comprehensive ultrasound examination, usually performed between weeks 13-16 of pregnancy. The purpose of the examination is to assess fetal structure at an early stage and systematically examine the developing body organs - including the brain, heart, spine, face, limbs, abdominal wall, kidneys, and additional systems.
The examination enables early identification of structural findings, assessment of fetal development relative to gestational age, and sometimes guidance for appropriate follow-up, monitoring, or counseling as needed.
At the clinic, the scan is performed with a thorough, sensitive, and professional approach, using particularly advanced ultrasound equipment that enables high-quality and clear imaging of the fetus even in early pregnancy stages. During the examination, emphasis is placed on clear explanation, complete transparency, and answering questions - with understanding that this is a significant and exciting examination for the parents.
The examination is suitable for women who want early, professional, and comprehensive assessment of fetal structure and development.
clinic_services_p_41: Examination of major fetal organs — heart, brain, spine, abdomen, and limbs — in the early stage of pregnancy
clinic_services_p_42: Assessment of growth rate and anatomical development relative to gestational age, using advanced ultrasound equipment
clinic_services_p_43: Early identification of abnormal structural findings enabling personalized follow-up planning and genetic counseling when needed
clinic_services_p_44:
Late anatomy scan is a comprehensive ultrasound examination, usually performed between weeks 20-24 of pregnancy. The purpose of the examination is to systematically assess fetal structure and body system development at a more advanced stage of pregnancy - including the brain, heart, spine, face, limbs, kidneys, digestive system, and additional organs.
The examination enables identification of structural findings that may appear or become clear only later in pregnancy, assessment of fetal growth, amniotic fluid quantity, placental location, and sometimes blood flow assessment according to clinical need.
At the clinic, the scan is performed with a professional, thorough, and sensitive approach, using particularly advanced ultrasound equipment that enables high-quality and clear imaging of the fetus. During the examination, emphasis is placed on clear, patient, and personalized explanation to give parents confidence and complete understanding of what is being examined.
The examination is suitable for women who want comprehensive and professional assessment of fetal structure and development in the second trimester of pregnancy.
clinic_services_p_45: Detailed review of all body systems — heart, lungs, kidneys, brain, face, skeleton, and additional structures according to international protocols from leading centers worldwide
clinic_services_p_46: At Life Inside clinic, we offer a range of obstetric ultrasound examinations at the highest level — from early pregnancy stages to delivery. Every examination is performed exclusively by Dr. Ron Harel, combining international expertise, advanced technology, and a personal and humane approach.
clinic_services_p_47: Private anatomy scan in Ra'anana for the third trimester of pregnancy, designed for in-depth examination of fetal growth rate, placental function, amniotic fluid quantity, and fetal well-being indicators. The examination enables early identification of intrauterine growth restriction, late anomalies, and conditions requiring close monitoring — all in a personal and calm environment.
clinic_services_p_48: Dr. Harel leads the field of fetal neurology in Israel and performs targeted fetal brain scans at the highest level worldwide, inspired by his experience at BCNatal Barcelona. These examinations are designed for women with findings requiring clarification, high-risk pregnancies, or any condition requiring accurate fetal neurological assessment.
clinic_services_p_49: Additionally, Life Inside clinic offers targeted scans for specific organs — heart, kidneys, face, and spine — alongside counseling and diagnosis in high-risk pregnancies requiring expert eyes.
clinic_services_p_50: In every visit, we maintain an organized, personal, and comprehensive process — so you leave with a clear picture and genuine peace of mind.
clinic_services_p_51: The appointment begins with a personal and in-depth conversation. Dr. Harak reviews your medical history, previous findings, and test results — to understand the complete picture before any examination.
clinic_services_p_52: A detailed and meticulous ultrasound examination, performed with advanced equipment and experienced hands. All fetal systems are carefully examined, with special attention to organs relevant to your pregnancy stage.
clinic_services_p_53: At the end of the examination, Dr. Harak explains the findings in accessible and understandable language, answers questions, and alleviates concerns. No finding is left unexplained — everything is explained openly and patiently.
clinic_services_p_54: Based on the findings, Dr. Harak formulates personalized recommendations — whether it involves continuing regular monitoring, referral for additional tests, or consultation regarding high-risk pregnancy management.
clinic_services_p_55: Every visit is conducted at your pace, without pressure and without compromising on quality — because every pregnancy is a world unto itself.
clinic_services_p_56: Every pregnancy is unique, and every stage requires precise care. Dr. Ron Harak will be happy to advise and customize the right examination for you — whether it's a private nuchal translucency screening in Ra'anana, private anatomy scan in Ra'anana, third trimester scan, or targeted examination in high-risk pregnancy — all under one roof, with personal and professional guidance.

targeted_review_h3_9: A targeted review is suitable for women who were referred following a finding in a previous ultrasound examination, abnormal or borderline results, suspected anatomical finding, need for monitoring a specific organ, or desire to receive additional assessment by a fetal medicine specialist.
targeted_review_p_15: Dr. Hark performs targeted reviews in various conditions requiring focused and thorough attention.
targeted_review_p_16: Answers to the most frequently asked questions about targeted review

clinic_services_h3_11: Third Trimester Scan — Growth and Development
clinic_services_h3_17: Third Trimester Scan — Growth and Fetal Well-being
clinic_services_h3_18: Targeted Scans — Fetal Brain and Focused Organs
targeted_review_h3_10: When a question arises regarding a specific finding, it is important to perform a focused and thorough assessment by a doctor with experience in the relevant field. A targeted review allows for diagnostic precision, distinguishing between normal variants and significant findings, and providing parents with clearer and more confident information for the continuation of pregnancy.
targeted_review_li_45:
Suspected clubfoot, placental or umbilical cord findings.

Suspected fetal malformation or structural problem

When there is suspicion of a defect in the brain, face, heart, spine, chest, abdomen, kidneys, limbs or other organ  a targeted examination of that system can be performed.

Need for targeted fetal brain examination

In cases of ventricular dilatation, suspected corpus callosum problems, cerebellar findings, brain cysts, suspected bleeding, infection, or other findings in the central nervous system.

Kidney and urinary tract findings

For example, renal pelvis dilatation, suspected urinary tract obstruction, enlarged bladder, absent kidney, ectopic kidney, kidney cysts or suspected abnormal renal structure.

Digestive system or abdominal wall findings

Such as bowel loop dilatation, echogenic bowel, suspected obstruction, liver, stomach or gallbladder findings, etc.

Skeletal and limb findings

For example, suspected long bone shortening, abnormal foot positioning, clubfoot, polydactyly or oligodactyly, hand findings, suspected skeletal deformities or spinal structure problems.

Placental, umbilical cord or amniotic fluid findings

Such as low-lying placenta or placenta previa, abnormal blood vessels, umbilical cord variations, single umbilical artery, polyhydramnios or oligohydramnios, or need for monitoring placental findings.

Growth restriction or suspected fetal growth problems

When there is suspicion of small for gestational age fetus, measurement discrepancies, growth rate deceleration or need for Doppler blood flow assessment.
Genetic finding or abnormal test result

When abnormal or inconclusive results are received from screening tests, NIPT, genetic chip, exome, amniocentesis or chorionic villus sampling - sometimes a targeted scan is required to search for structural findings related to the result.

Infection exposure during pregnancy

In cases of suspected or diagnosed maternal infection during pregnancy, such as CMV, toxoplasmosis or other infections, there may be a need for targeted assessment of the brain, liver, intestines, placenta, amniotic fluid and fetal growth.

Underlying diseases or medications in pregnancy

In certain conditions of maternal diseases, diabetes, epilepsy, autoimmune diseases, taking certain medications or special exposures during pregnancy - a targeted examination may be recommended according to the medical question.

rear_transparency_h1_2: What is the Nuchal Translucency Test?
rear_transparency_h2_5: Schedule an Appointment for Nuchal Translucency Test
rear_transparency_h3_6: The nuchal translucency test is an important ultrasound examination performed in the first trimester of pregnancy, usually between week 11+0 and week 13+6. During the examination, the thickness of fluid in the fetal neck area is measured, a measurement that helps assess the risk for certain chromosomal syndromes, including Down syndrome, Trisomy 18 and Trisomy 13.
rear_transparency_h3_7: Beyond measuring the translucency itself, the examination allows early assessment of gestational age, fetal heartbeat, number of fetuses, and sometimes also an initial impression of major anatomical structures. When the examination is performed by an experienced physician with advanced ultrasound equipment, significant information about pregnancy development can be obtained at an early stage.
rear_transparency_h3_8: Important to know: Nuchal translucency is a screening test - meaning it does not definitively diagnose a chromosomal problem, but rather assesses the risk level. The final risk calculation is determined after completing blood tests of the first trimester and second trimester biochemical screening. NIPT testing, genetic chip testing, exome testing or additional diagnostic tests can also be performed.
rear_transparency_h3_9: At Dr. Harak's clinic, the nuchal translucency test is performed with a professional, calm and personal approach, using advanced ultrasound equipment and high accuracy, to provide parents with clear, reliable and reassuring information from the very beginning of the journey.
rear_transparency_p_14: The information about the scope and nature of the examination is based on accepted guidelines in the field of fetal medicine, including FMF and ACOG.
rear_transparency_p_15: The nuchal translucency test is not a diagnostic test — it assesses the risk level and is combined with additional findings and blood tests to create an accurate risk profile.
rear_transparency_p_16: Dr. Ron Hark — Life Inside. Professional, warm and accurate guidance throughout pregnancy. Contact us today to schedule your examination.
rear_transparency_p_17: Dr. Ron Hark | The Life Before Life
targeted_review_alt_62: Dr. Ron Harkh performing targeted scan

targeted_review_btn_41: Who refers for a targeted scan?
targeted_review_btn_42: Can a targeted scan be scheduled without a referral?
targeted_review_btn_43: How long does the examination take?
targeted_review_btn_44: What happens if a finding is discovered?
targeted_review_div_47: A targeted scan is usually required based on a referral from an obstetrician, geneticist or genetic counselor. The referral usually comes after a suspicious finding in a morphology scan, serological test, or following a genetic test that revealed a finding requiring sonographic verification
targeted_review_div_48: At Life Inside clinic, you can also come on your own initiative, but it is recommended to arrive with relevant medical documentation.  Previous test results, physician summary, or findings from a previous scan. This information allows Dr. Charak to perform the examination in the most targeted and efficient way.
targeted_review_div_49: The duration of the targeted scan depends on the type of examination and complexity of the finding. Usually the examination lasts between 60 to 90 minutes, including consultation time before and after.
targeted_review_div_50: In case of a finding, Dr. Harkh explains the clinical significance clearly and sensitively, and refers to appropriate care providers — geneticist, pediatric cardiologist, fetal neurologist or any relevant specialist. The written summary is forwarded to the referring physician, and you can always contact us for additional questions even after the examination.

preliminary_review_h2_2: What is the preliminary systems review?
preliminary_review_h2_4: Ready to schedule an appointment for a preliminary review?
preliminary_review_h3_5: The preliminary systems review is an important and comprehensive ultrasound examination, usually performed between weeks 13-17 of pregnancy. The purpose of the examination is to assess the fetal structure at an early stage, and to systematically examine the developing body organs: including the brain, heart, spine, face, limbs, abdominal wall, kidneys and other systems.
preliminary_review_h3_6:
The main advantage of the preliminary review is the ability to identify some findings at a relatively early stage of pregnancy. In cases where a finding requiring further investigation is discovered, timely referral to genetic counseling, additional tests, targeted review or appropriate follow-up can be made.

The examination is non-invasive and does not endanger the mother or fetus.

It is important to know that even when the review is normal, it is not possible to completely rule out all malformations, syndromes or developmental problems. Some findings may develop or be discovered only at later stages of pregnancy, therefore the preliminary review does not replace the late review and continued pregnancy monitoring.
preliminary_review_h3_7: At the clinic, the review is performed with a thorough, sensitive and professional approach, using particularly advanced ultrasound equipment that enables high-quality and clear imaging of the fetus even in the early stages of pregnancy.
preliminary_review_h3_8: During the examination, emphasis is placed on clear explanation, complete transparency and answering questions, with understanding that this is a significant and exciting examination for the parents. The examination is suitable for women interested in an early, professional and comprehensive assessment of fetal structure and development.
preliminary_review_p_12: Contact Life Inside clinic in Ra'anana to schedule an appointment with Dr. Ron Harel. We are here to answer any question and provide you with peace of mind throughout your pregnancy.

rear_transparency_alt_62: Life Inside Clinic – Beit Manzur Ra'anana
rear_transparency_btn_37: If the translucency is high — what do we do?
rear_transparency_btn_38: Is it possible to determine the fetal gender
rear_transparency_btn_40: Can nuchal translucency be combined with early screening?
rear_transparency_div_46: A finding of high translucency is not a diagnosis, it indicates an increased risk that requires further investigation. Dr. Hark will guide you regarding the next steps: NIPT test, amniocentesis, or chorionic villus sampling (CVS) — according to the risk level and gestational week.
rear_transparency_div_47:
Yes - sometimes it's possible to get an initial assessment of the fetal gender already during the nuchal translucency test, mainly according to the angle of the genital tubercle, but it's important to know that at this stage it's only an assessment and not a definitive determination.
 At weeks 11-13+6 the external genitalia are still in developmental stages. Therefore, even when there's a hint in a certain direction, the accuracy level depends greatly on the gestational week, fetal position, imaging quality, and the ability to see the area clearly.
 Usually, as we approach week 13-14 a more accurate assessment can be obtained, but clearer confirmation is usually given during the early anatomy scan.
rear_transparency_div_48: Ultrasound findings are received immediately during the examination itself. With the test results, we proceed to complete the biochemical screening within the framework of the insuring health fund.
rear_transparency_div_49: It's possible to combine the nuchal translucency test with early anatomical assessment of the fetus, especially around weeks 13–14. This combination allows obtaining important information about fetal development at an early stage, alongside measuring translucency and risk assessment. However, since some fetal systems are still in developmental stages, completion of this examination may be required and of course it's essential to also perform a late scan at weeks 21-24.

preliminary_review_btn_33: Does the preliminary review replace the late review?
preliminary_review_btn_34: Is the examination performed vaginally or abdominally?
preliminary_review_btn_35: How long does the examination take?
preliminary_review_btn_36: What if a finding is discovered?
preliminary_review_div_38: No. The preliminary review does not replace the late systems review (weeks 21-24). It constitutes a complementary examination that allows earlier assessment of fetal structure. It is recommended to perform both reviews to obtain a complete and comprehensive picture of fetal health.
preliminary_review_div_39: Depends on the week of pregnancy. In weeks 13-15, a vaginal examination is required. After week 16, sometimes an abdominal examination may suffice.
preliminary_review_div_40: The examination usually lasts between 45-60 minutes, depending on the fetus's cooperation and position. Dr. Harel dedicates the time required for a comprehensive and accurate examination, and explains the findings at the end of the examination.
preliminary_review_div_41: In case of a finding, Dr. Harel will explain the significance clearly and professionally, and refer to appropriate follow-up tests such as amniocentesis, genetic tests, fetal echocardiography or increased monitoring. Early detection allows more options and more time for making informed decisions.

first_pregnancy_ultrasound_h3_6: The first examination that confirms everything is starting properly. Using gentle transvaginal ultrasound, we locate the gestational sac and verify the presence of normal fetal heartbeat.

first_pregnancy_ultrasound_p_14: The examination is usually performed transvaginally and is typically painless. Dr. Charak ensures patient comfort throughout the entire examination.
first_pregnancy_ultrasound_p_15: For transvaginal examination, it's recommended to arrive with an empty bladder (use the restroom before the examination). No fasting or other special preparation is needed. You may bring your partner to experience the moment together.
first_pregnancy_ultrasound_p_16: The examination itself takes about 15 minutes, and including consultation, explanations and answering questions – about 20-30 minutes total.
first_pregnancy_ultrasound_p_17: There are several possible reasons for not detecting a heartbeat, and it's not always a concerning finding. Dr. Charak will explain all possibilities clearly and sensitively, determine appropriate follow-up, and be available for any questions.

third_trimester_ultrasound_h1_1: Third Trimester Review for Fetal Development Assessment
third_trimester_ultrasound_h2_2: What is the Third Trimester Review?
third_trimester_ultrasound_h2_3: Who is the Third Trimester Review Recommended For?
third_trimester_ultrasound_h2_4: Frequently Asked Questions About the Third Trimester Review
third_trimester_ultrasound_h2_5: Ready to Schedule an Appointment for the Third Trimester Review?
third_trimester_ultrasound_h3_6:
The third trimester review is an advanced ultrasound examination usually performed in the third trimester of pregnancy, typically around weeks 30-34. The purpose of the examination is to assess fetal development at a more advanced stage of pregnancy, after organs have grown and matured, and to identify findings that could not always be seen in earlier stages. During the examination, fetal structure, growth, amniotic fluid volume, placental position, fetal movements, and sometimes blood flow with Doppler are examined, according to clinical need. Special attention is given to systems that may change or manifest later in pregnancy, such as the brain, kidneys, digestive system, heart, and skeleton. The third trimester review does not replace previous reviews, but provides an additional layer of security - an examination that allows a renewed and precise look at the fetus at a stage when some findings may be clearer. 

At the clinic, the examination is performed with a thorough, calm, and personal approach, using advanced ultrasound equipment that enables high-quality and clear imaging, with detailed explanations throughout and after the examination.
third_trimester_ultrasound_h3_7:
Who is the third trimester scan suitable for? The examination is suitable for women interested in additional and comprehensive assessment in the third trimester, as well as in
situations where there is a need to follow up on a previous finding, growth assessment, suspected changes in amniotic fluid volume, high-risk pregnancy, or desire to receive a current and more comprehensive picture towards the continuation of pregnancy and delivery.
third_trimester_ultrasound_p_11:
Why perform a third trimester scan?
 Because even after normal early and late scans, there are certain systems and findings that may develop or manifest only later in pregnancy. A third trimester scan allows us to look again, at the right time, with a professional eye, and with advanced technology  to provide parents with additional information and confidence towards the final stages of pregnancy.
third_trimester_ultrasound_p_12: The third trimester review is especially recommended for pregnant women with risk factors, but can be performed for any woman interested in reassurance and comprehensive information before delivery.
third_trimester_ultrasound_p_14: Comprehensive and professional examination by a specialist, with answers to every question and peace of mind before delivery.

third_trimester_ultrasound_li_44: High blood pressure in pregnancy / preeclampsia — conditions that can impair blood flow to the placenta and affect fetal growth
third_trimester_ultrasound_li_45: Gestational diabetes — can cause an overly large fetus (macrosomia) or damage to the placenta
third_trimester_ultrasound_li_46: Multiple pregnancy (twins and above) — risk of growth discrepancies requiring comparison and monitoring between each fetus
third_trimester_ultrasound_li_47: Suspected intrauterine growth restriction (IUGR) — the scan confirms the diagnosis and helps determine treatment policy

third_trimester_ultrasound_btn_40: Does every woman need a third trimester scan?
third_trimester_ultrasound_btn_41: What is IUGR and how is it treated?
third_trimester_ultrasound_btn_42: What exactly does the Doppler check?
third_trimester_ultrasound_btn_43: When exactly is the examination performed?
third_trimester_ultrasound_div_49: The third trimester scan is not mandatory for all pregnant women, but is highly recommended for women with risk factors. However, many women choose to perform it even without special risk factors, to get a complete picture of the fetus's condition before delivery and to alleviate anxieties. Dr. Hark recommends discussing the matter during a routine pregnancy visit and making an informed decision according to personal circumstances.
third_trimester_ultrasound_div_50: IUGR (Intrauterine Growth Restriction) – intrauterine growth restriction – is a condition where the fetus is not growing at the expected rate for the gestational week. The causes can be poor placental function, maternal diseases, genetic factors and more. Treatment depends on the severity of the condition and gestational age: increased monitoring with frequent Doppler tests, rest, and in severe cases – planned early delivery. Early diagnosis through the third trimester scan enables rapid response and preservation of fetal health.
third_trimester_ultrasound_div_51: Doppler examination measures the speed and direction of blood flow in specific blood vessels. In the third trimester scan, we mainly check: the umbilical artery – reflecting placental resistance; the middle cerebral artery (MCA) – indicating the fetal condition and its ability to maintain blood flow to the brain; and sometimes liver veins as well. Abnormal Doppler values can precede changes in heart rate and assist in making early treatment decisions.
third_trimester_ultrasound_div_52: The third trimester scan is usually performed between week 28 and week 34 of pregnancy. The preferred timing is around weeks 30–32, as at this stage reliable measurements of fetal growth can be obtained and there is still time to act if necessary. In women with risk factors, additional follow-up examinations may be performed at higher frequency, according to the doctor's recommendation.
